statement.en: A protein language model unveils the E. coli pangenome functional landscape regulating host proteostasis
statement.summary: Extended Data for the Article: A protein language model unveils the E. coli pangenome functional landscape regulating host proteostasis Summary: libraries-barcodes: barcodes and associated metadata used to demultiplex the C. elegans RNA-seq for the mono-association experiments between E. coli strains and C. elegans RNA_lexogen_worm_norm_batch: DESeq2 normalised and batch corrected counts for the RNA-seq data experiment. MAIN_ecoli_NCBI_metadata: complete metadata of the 9558 E. coli strains used in the article. gene_presence_absence.Rtab: Panaroo-generated file with the presence/absence matrix of the 9558 strains pan_genome_reference(_aa)_TOTAL: Panaroo-generated file with the linear reference of the nucleotide or amino acid sequences of the gene families found in the pangenome gene_t5_emb

proposer: Daniel Martínez-Martínez, Andreea Aprodu, Cassandra Backes et al.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: This is a preprint (2026-04-25) presenting empirical bioinformatics research with protein language models applied to E. coli pangenomics. The claims are data-driven and testable, but validation status is uncertain without peer review publication. The work combines established ML techniques (protein embeddings via T5) with novel application domain (pangenome-proteostasis regulation), placing it in provisional-but-substantive territory.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 数千の菌株の言語が、宿主の蛋白質調和を規定する隠れた周波数で共鳴する。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 9558の菌株が一つの原型蛋白質言語に折り畳まれ、宿主との奏でる共鳴が浮かぶ。
